channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests arrest warrants for the head of Myanmar's military regime
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor asked judges on Wednesday to issue an arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar’s military regime for crimes committed against the country’s Rohingya Muslim minority. Senior Gen.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Germany's Merkel recalls Putin's 'power games' and contrasting US presidents in her memoirs
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel recalls Vladimir Putin's “power games” over the years, remembers contrasting meetings with Barack Obama and Donald Trump and says she asked herself whether she could have done more to prevent Brexit, in her memoirs published Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A Catholic family's answer to opposing abortion: adopt, foster and vote
item.description: SUNBURY, Ohio (AP) — For the Young family in rural Sunbury, Ohio, activism begins at home. The conservative Catholic family chooses to live their anti-abortion beliefs through adoption, foster-parenting and raising their children to believe in the sanctity of life.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN KASTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 takeaways from Trump's health agency nominations
item.description: Almost all have been highly critical of the agencies they would run if confirmed, and experts say the nominees represent a complete overhaul of the vision and priorities of key agencies for health care and public health.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Venezuelan gang members arrested in southern state same week officials warned of Tren de Aragua resurgence
item.description: Tennessee authorities arrested two members of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua in different cities last week, when officials issued a warning about TDA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC woman released from prison after new evidence contradicts accounts claiming she helped hitman in 1999
item.description: A New York woman who had been in prison for 13 years was released after new evidence contradicted accounts that she helped a hitman carry out a murder 25 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California coastal enclave near sanctuary city tries to 'Newsom-proof' itself
item.description: A California mayor criticized state lawmakers for the sanctuary city policies he alleges have exacerbated illegal immigration into the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Long-sought court ruling restores Oregon tribe's hunting and fishing rights
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — Drumming made the floor vibrate and singing filled the conference room of the Chinook Winds Casino Resort in Lincoln City, on the Oregon coast, as hundreds in tribal regalia danced in a circle.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump promised mass deportations. Educators worry fear will keep immigrants' kids from school
item.description: Regardless of whether President-elect Donald Trump follows through on his pledge to deport millions of immigrants, educators around the U.S. are bracing for upheaval.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Trump taps Kevin Hassett as National Economic Council head
item.description: President-elect Trump has chosen former White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett to lead the National Economic Council in his next administration, he said Tuesday. “I am very pleased to announce that Dr. Kevin A.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Fired workers blamed for knocking over 120 headstones at Tennessee cemetery
item.description: More than 120 headstones at a Tennessee cemetery were knocked over by freshly fired employees, according to the cemetery's owner.
item.dc:creator: Shay Arthur, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California man allegedly checked suitcases containing meth-caked clothes and a cow pajama onesie: DOJ
item.description: A California man faces federal charges after he allegedly checked two suitcases into Los Angeles International Airport that contained clothing caked in methamphetamine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida TikTok influencer arrested for stealing $500 worth of items at Target: police
item.description: Florida police have arrested a TikTok influencer after she was caught on Target security footage allegedly stealing $500 worth of items.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tank explodes, catches fire near GM facility in Michigan
item.description: A tank containing byproducts from oil drilling operations exploded and caught fire Tuesday near the General Motors Proving Ground in southeastern Michigan, damaging two nearby homes but causing no injuries, a fire chief said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Police say FBI agent sexually assaulted 2 women after promise of free tattoos, modeling
item.description: GAITHERSBURG, Md. (AP) — An FBI agent charged in Maryland with sexually assaulting two women contacted his alleged victims through social media with a promise to give them free tattoos and modeling work, police said Tuesday as they encouraged other potential victims to come forward.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How Trump's bet on voters electing him managed to silence some of his legal woes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One year after the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol attack, Attorney General Merrick Garland said the Justice Department was committed to holding accountable all perpetrators “at any level” for “the assault on our democracy.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump vows tariffs over immigration. What the numbers say about border crossings, drugs and crime
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is threatening tariffs on Mexico and Canada as he seeks to portray them as responsible for illegal immigration and drug smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walmart's DEI rollback signals a profound shift in the wake of Trump's election victory
item.description: Walmart’s sweeping rollback of its diversity policies is the strongest indication yet of a profound shift taking hold at U.S. companies that are re-evaluating the legal and political risks associated with bold programs to bolster historically underrepresented groups in business.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson And Cathy Bussewitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: San Francisco watchdog Ricci Wynne arrested, suspected of pimping
item.description: Wynne has legions of social media followers. Over the past three years, videos posted on his social media pages, RawRicci415, garnered hundreds of thousands of views.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: San Francisco watchdog Ricci Wynne arrested, suspected of pimping
item.description: Wynne has legions of social media followers. Over the past three years, videos posted on his social media pages, RawRicci415, garnered hundreds of thousands of views.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Pursuit suspect, police engage in shootout; multiple people detained
item.description: Authorities were pursuing the driver of a fleeing Maserati near West Adams in Los Angeles Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: DEA seizes 670K fentanyl pills headed to Denver
item.description: The Drug Enforcement Agency said Monday that a tip led to the seizure of around 670,000 fake pills headed to Denver from Albuquerque.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Thanksgiving Travel Latest: TSA braces for ‘busiest ever’ holiday
item.description: The Thanksgiving travel rush is expected to be bigger than ever this year. AAA predicts that nearly 80 million people in the U.S. will venture at least 50 miles (80 kilometers) from home between Tuesday and next Monday. Another round of wintry weather could complicate travel, though.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Garance Burke And Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Uber driver killed in road rage shooting on Las Vegas Strip
item.description: An Uber driver is dead after he was shot in the neck during a road rage incident on the Las Vegas Strip.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: 3 men accused of climbing to the top of Nashville's AT&T building
item.description: "One of the men told officers that they had heard the AT&T Building was the best place to get a view of Nashville," police said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia pilot, dog killed in New York plane crash; 2 other dogs found alive
item.description: A Virginia pilot and a rescue dog he was transporting were killed after a plane he was flying crashed in New York on Sunday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, WGN 9

item.title: You can get paid $2,500 to shop at Costco for a week
item.description: Attention Costco shoppers! How would you like to get paid $2,500 plus a free Costco membership to shop at your favorite store?
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of Pennsylvania teen at center of 1973 cold case identified as investigation continues
item.description: Ruth Elizabeth Brenneman was last seen leaving for school more than 50 years ago. Now, her remains have been identified as the investigation into her disappearance continues.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9

item.title: Russell and Hamilton give Mercedes a 1-2 finish in Las Vegas
item.description: An ecstatic George Russell had just won the Formula 1 race Saturday night when he exclaimed in his radio, “Vegas!” He was ready to start celebrating in America's party capital, saying he would skip his flight back to his home in the United Kingdom to make the most of his Las Vegas Grand Prix victory
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Will a winter storm hit the US over Thanksgiving week? Here's what forecasts show so far
item.description: WINDSOR, Calif. (AP) — Forecasters warned over the weekend that another round of winter weather could complicate travel leading up to Thanksgiving in parts of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GODOFREDO A. VASQUEZ and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's unveils new value menu, extends $5 Meal Deal
item.description: The fast-food giant announced on Friday that it will launch a new everyday value menu on Jan. 7, 2025, which will include exclusive in-app offers, a new Buy One, Add One offer, and the popular $5 Meal Deal.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: 2 convicted in human smuggling case after Indian family froze to death on US-Canada border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A jury convicted two men on Friday of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S. border during a 2022 blizzard.
item.dc:creator: MARK VANCLEAVE and MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, ABC 4
